description: Gold questions engineered to expose vector RAG's weaknesses vs graph RAG. Each needs facts scattered across many chunks/episodes, or traversal/aggregation that top-k similarity cannot assemble.

questions.question: List every character Arya Stark kills.
questions.why_vector_struggles: Kills are spread across many episodes; top-k returns only the few most similar scenes, so the list is always partial.

questions.question: How is Jon Snow connected to Tywin Lannister?
questions.why_vector_struggles: The link is a chain of relationships never stated in any single chunk; similarity search has no notion of a path.

questions.question: How many named characters belong to House Lannister?
questions.why_vector_struggles: Counting requires ALL members; retrieval caps at top-k chunks, so the count is unknowable from a few passages.

questions.question: Which characters have held the title Hand of the King?
questions.why_vector_struggles: Different holders appear in different seasons; no single chunk enumerates them.

questions.question: Which characters betrayed someone they were sworn to?
questions.why_vector_struggles: Requires joining SWORN_TO facts with BETRAYED facts across the corpus — a relational join, not a similarity lookup.

questions.question: What is the shortest relationship path between Arya Stark and Cersei Lannister?
questions.why_vector_struggles: A graph path query with no textual equivalent to retrieve.

questions.question: Who killed Tywin Lannister, and how is that killer related to Cersei Lannister?
questions.why_vector_struggles: The killer fact and the sibling fact sit in different scenes; top-k rarely returns both and cannot chain them into one answer.

questions.question: List every character Tyrion Lannister kills.
questions.why_vector_struggles: Kills are scattered across episodes; similarity returns only the few most-similar scenes, so the list is always partial.

questions.question: What is the full relationship between Petyr Baelish and Lysa Arryn?
questions.why_vector_struggles: Two distinct relations on one pair; retrieval blends them into fuzzy prose and often misses that he both married and killed her.

questions.question: Who is Ramsay Bolton's father, and which house does that father belong to?
questions.why_vector_struggles: The two-hop parent-then-house link is never stated in any single chunk.

questions.question: Who killed the Mad King Aerys II Targaryen, and what group and house was the killer part of?
questions.graph_path: Jaime -KILLED-> Aerys II ; Jaime -MEMBER_OF_GROUP-> Kingsguard ; Jaime -MEMBER_OF_HOUSE-> House Lannister
questions.why_vector_struggles: Directional killer->victim fact plus an allegiance join; similarity search has no notion of direction or path.

questions.question: Why does Tyrion Lannister kill his father Tywin?
questions.why_graph_struggles: The graph stores the KILLED edge but not the motive (finding Shae in Tywin's bed, the Tysha wound), which lives only in the passage text.

questions.question: Describe how the Battle of Blackwater unfolds.
questions.why_graph_struggles: An Event node carries no scene-level narrative; the blow-by-blow detail sits in adjacent chunks that embedding similarity retrieves well.

questions.question: What does Melisandre mean when she says 'the night is dark and full of terrors'?
questions.why_graph_struggles: A quote/theme lookup with no representation as any ontology relation.

questions.question: How does Arya Stark's relationship with the Hound change as they travel together?
questions.why_graph_struggles: An evolving emotional arc expressed only in prose; no schema relation captures shifting attitude.

questions.question: What happens at Daenerys's wedding to Khal Drogo, and what gifts does she receive?
questions.why_graph_struggles: The graph has only the bare MARRIED_TO edge; the descriptive detail (the dragon eggs and other gifts) lives in a single passage.
